chunks.content:
Article: A question on Rei
[]: A question on Rei             Quote Post by Leighton » Thu Feb 17, 2011 11:20 am Sorry if this question seems naive, but how do the 3 Rei clones in the series each retain that single soul? How is it transferred to Rei III after Rei II blows herself up? I see that there is some muddle up in her memory or (maybe a progressive change in preferences to Shiji's desires?) , i.e. she shows some sort of disliking or opposition to Gendo by breaking his glasses and then later, ultimately betraying his complementation project. Being that there is only one "Rei soul", how are they transferred between the incarnated clones? Even though there are many spare "vessels", the one given the Rei soul, has a personality. How do they retain this soul for the next incarnation?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Feb 17, 2011 11:49 am In #23', after Rei 2 self destructs, Ritsuko is sent to retrieve her body from the entry plug. #23 wrote: EYECATCH B: EPISODE #23': Rei III ***** MAN (OFF): No antibiotic substance from the 16th Angel has been found thus far. We will continue searching. MAN (OFF): Level C traces are spreading towards the southwest. MAN: Dr. Akagi! RITSUKO: What happened here will be classified top secret. Take back the plug. Dispose of everything else. MAN: Roger that. MAN: Let's get to work! Quickly!

[]:             Quote Post by Leighton » Thu Feb 17, 2011 12:03 pm Ahh, very nice explanation. Its quite fortunate that they managed to salvage the remains/soul after that eva suicide. I think i have a clearer image as to how the Rei's are reincarnated. Thanks! :)
